summary: Re-ran full penalty pytest suite after fix to _require_auth in /app/backend/amount/penalty_views.py. Result: 10/10 PASSED in 3.48s. The previously failing 401-vs-403 tests (test_summary_requires_auth, test_recompute_requires_auth, test_pending_requires_auth) now all pass. All other behaviour from iteration 1 (login, summary shape, rate_per_month=25, idempotent recompute with updated=0 on second call, pending list shape, penalty_amount == 25 * missed_months, rate stability, legacy /api/user/admins_view/ 200) remains green. No regressions.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix is minimal and well-scoped: try/except AuthenticationFailed -> 401 in _require_auth. Good.

Still open from iteration 1 (non-blocking): token_app.views.token_checking returns mixed types (User instance OR DRF Response) and uses a bare 'except:' that maps to HTTP 417. Consider narrowing the except and standardising the contract to raise AuthenticationFailed consistently.
penalty_engine.recompute_all() still evaluates qs twice (iter + qs.count()); minor perf opportunity for larger datasets.
_due_date(p) is called twice per row inside _serialize_item path; cache locally for readability.
test_credentials: admin@gmail.com / Admin@123 (jwt sent raw, no 'Bearer ' prefix).
seed_data_creation: None - used existing MySQL temple DB. Verified total_pending_penalty > 0 and idempotent recompute (updated=0 on 2nd call).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Penalty backend is fully green. Curl sanity (no Auth header) confirms 401 on all three endpoints: /api/penalty/summary/, /api/penalty/pending/, /api/penalty/recompute/. Suite path: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_penalty_api.py -v. Auth header format is raw JWT (no 'Bearer '). External URL: https://fd32960a-5552-41c8-bee2-e9a5572be60a.preview.emergentagent.com. Frontend not yet tested for penalty UI - main agent may request that next.
rca of the issue: Previous failure RCA was: token_checking raises rest_framework.exceptions.AuthenticationFailed and DRF mapped it to 403 because @api_view had no authenticator providing a WWW-Authenticate header. Fix applied by main agent: wrap token_checking(request) call inside _require_auth with try/except AuthenticationFailed and return Response({'message':'Not Authorized'}, status=401). Verified by both pytest suite (10/10 pass) and direct curl (all three endpoints return HTTP 401 with no Authorization header).
